contentModelDetail.notes: Block Collection hero. Adapt CSS for Almac brand gradient overlays. Carousel variant wraps multiple hero rows in a carousel block for homepage.
Block Collection cards. Add variant CSS for news (date+title), resource (overlay gradient), event (date+location+CTA), related (compact row), icon (small icon grid). Each card = 1 row.
Block Collection columns used as-is for media-text split layouts. Video variant has thumbnail + play icon that triggers modal.

rows.col1: Slide content (varies: hero content or card content)

contentModelDetail.notes: Block Collection Carousel wraps each row as a slide. Homepage hero slides use carousel(hero) with nested hero content. Events page uses carousel for featured events. Replaces Slick.js.
Block Collection Video for direct video playback. Vimeo is primary provider. Modal variant triggers overlay playback.
Block Collection Embed auto-detects provider. Acts as autoblock for bare URLs on their own line. Used for Google Maps on contact page and Vimeo/YouTube fallbacks.
Block Collection Quote for individual testimonials. For testimonial carousels, wrap multiple quote blocks inside a carousel block.
Block Collection Accordion. Each row = one expandable panel. Light usage across site.

rows.col2: Tab content (text, images, or fragment reference)

contentModelDetail.notes: Block Collection Tabs. Used on some service pages for grouping capabilities by phase/category.
Auto-blocked: injected via scripts.js based on URL path hierarchy. No author table needed. Generate breadcrumb from URL segments, mapping slug to human-readable labels via metadata or a lookup.

Custom block. Loads HubSpot Forms JS API (lazy-loaded in delayed.js for performance). Portal ID + Form ID per instance. Site-wide 8-field form should be a Fragment (/fragments/hubspot-cta) included on most pages to avoid repetition.

fragmentStrategy.notes: The 8-field site-wide form is identical across ~3500 pages. Author it once as a Fragment. Page-specific forms (gated-content, product-inquiry) are authored inline.

contentModelDetail.notes: Custom block. Each row = one stat (number + label). Animated counter on scroll is a progressive enhancement in JS (IntersectionObserver). CSS handles responsive row layout.

Custom block for the profile header. Bio text, expertise lists, and related content sections below the profile block are default content. BD-team variant adds region/territory field. Expert variant adds credentials.

Block Collection Fragment. References a separate page whose content is inlined. Key fragments to create: /fragments/hubspot-cta (site-wide form), /fragments/footer-cta (if common CTA differs from form).
Block Collection Search. Integrated in header and/or a dedicated search results page. Works with query-index.json.

blockPalette.name: content-filter

contentModelDetail.notes: Most complex custom block. Configuration table specifies query-index sheet and filter fields. JS fetches from query-index.json, applies client-side filtering, renders card results with pagination. Block Party Content Filter Hub is the reference starting point. Must support URL-based filter state for bookmarkable/shareable filtered views.

blockPalette.riskNotes: Serves 2,330+ listing pages (events+news+knowledge). Requires well-structured query-index with correct metadata columns. Performance risk with large datasets. Build and test with events archetype first, then extend to news and knowledge.

contentModelDetail.notes: Block Collection Modal autoblock. Links to #hash trigger modal overlay. Video modals link to fragment page with embedded video. Form modals link to fragment with HubSpot form.
Block Collection Table. First row = headers. Used for product specifications on api-chemical-product pages.

eliminatedBlocks.name: contact-locations
eliminatedBlocks.reason: Compose from Columns + Fragment (HubSpot form) + Cards (location cards). Only 1 page (contact). Not worth a dedicated block.

eliminatedBlocks.name: icon-grid
eliminatedBlocks.reason: Implement as Cards (icon) variant with CSS. Only ~10 pages on clinical-technologies. Keeps palette minimal.

eliminatedBlocks.name: sidebar
eliminatedBlocks.reason: Implemented as a section style ('style: sidebar') using CSS Grid, not as a block. The sidebar content is authored as normal blocks/default content within a sidebar-styled section.

sectionStyles.name: sidebar
sectionStyles.css: CSS Grid 2fr 1fr desktop, stack on mobile

sectionStyles.name: full-width-image
sectionStyles.css: Background image cover, text overlay center/bottom

sectionStyles.name: cta-band
sectionStyles.css: Accent background, centered text, button styling

homepage.description: Site homepage with hero carousel, stats, cards grids, parallax image, and HubSpot form
homepage.migrationNotes: Hero carousel replaces Slick.js. Stats block has animated counters. Multiple card grids filtered by type (news, events, resources). Parallax map section becomes full-width-image section style with CTA overlay.

about.description: Corporate information pages with hero, media-text columns, video, cards, and form
about.migrationNotes: Standard division landing pattern. Some about pages have deeper content with multiple columns sections.

landing.description: Campaign/response landing pages with flexible content structure
landing.migrationNotes: Highly variable structure. Some pages have video embeds, some have cards. Migration should extract what's present and skip missing sections.

events.description: Event listing hub with content-filter and event archive. Individual event detail pages with date, location, description.
events.migrationNotes: 928 pages: ~5 are listing/archive pages using content-filter, ~923 are individual event detail pages. Detail pages are primarily default content with date/location metadata. Listing pages are the complex ones needing content-filter block. Individual event pages follow a simpler pattern: hero + breadcrumbs + default content (date, location, description, link) + form.

knowledge-library.description: Knowledge resource detail pages (whitepapers, webinars, publications) with gated content form and expert profile

sequences: default content (heading + body text + inline images)

knowledge-library.migrationNotes: Many pages are gated — require form submission to view content. Expert profile section links to knowledge-expert page. ~200 pages have internationalized (CJK) URL slugs requiring careful slug handling.

news-post.description: Individual news articles with sidebar navigation

sequences: default content (heading + date + body text + images)
| sidebar: default content (navigation links + category filters)

news-post.migrationNotes: Two-column layout with article body and sidebar. Sidebar contains sub-site navigation and category links. News articles span all business units — the division metadata field identifies which unit.

clinical-services.description: Clinical supply chain service pages with hero, animated intro, video, full-width images, and cards
clinical-services.migrationNotes: Sub-site with own WP install. Animated typewriter intro becomes default content (animation dropped or reimplemented as CSS animation). Full-width image sections use section style. 210 pages include sub-service detail pages.

diagnostics.description: Diagnostic services pages with hero, video, testimonials
diagnostics.migrationNotes: Sub-site. Testimonial carousel with quote blocks wrapped in carousel. Some pages have deep sub-navigation.

careers.description: Careers pages with hero, sidebar navigation, and career content
careers.migrationNotes: Careers section has its own sidebar navigation. Content is primarily default content with images. Job listings are a separate archetype.

knowledge-expert.migrationNotes: Profile block for photo+name+title header. Bio and expertise are default content below. Related content cards auto-populated from query-index. Gated content form for expert-specific resources.

clinical-technologies.description: Clinical technology service pages with icon grid, cards, and content sections
clinical-technologies.migrationNotes: Sub-site. Icon grid for capabilities becomes cards(icon) variant. Multiple card grids for news, events, resources. Hub page has many sections; detail pages are simpler.

api-chemical.migrationNotes: Sub-site. Service pages follow standard division pattern. Some pages have testimonial quotes in dark sections.

api-chemical-product.description: Biocatalyst product detail pages with sidebar, specs table, and inquiry form
api-chemical-product.migrationNotes: Product detail pages with sidebar navigation for product categories. Table block for product specifications. 19-field HubSpot inquiry form. No hero banner on these pages.

award.migrationNotes: Simple pages. Primarily default content with award description and image. No hero, no complex blocks. Most minimal archetype.

commercial-services.migrationNotes: Sub-site. Same pattern as clinical-services/pharma-drug. Full-width image parallax section.

analytical.description: Analytical & solid-state service pages with testimonials
analytical.migrationNotes: Sub-site. Testimonial section uses quote blocks in a carousel. Some pages have gated content (11-field) HubSpot forms.

careers-story.description: Almac Journeys employee story pages with sidebar

sequences: default content (heading + employee story text + images)
| sidebar: default content (careers navigation + share links)

careers-story.migrationNotes: Simple article-style pages with sidebar. No hero. Default content dominates. Sidebar has careers section navigation.

careers-resource.description: Gated careers resource pages — fully gated content
careers-resource.migrationNotes: FULLY GATED — all content behind HubSpot form. Sample scrape returned empty media. Migrate with stub content (title + gating form). Actual content may need manual review post-migration.

bd-team-profile.migrationNotes: Profile block for photo+name+region header. Bio is default content. Events cards show upcoming events for this BD rep.

pharma-drug.description: Pharmaceutical drug product development service pages
pharma-drug.migrationNotes: Sub-site. Identical layout pattern to clinical-services and commercial-services.

discovery.migrationNotes: Sub-site. Smaller division with fewer content sections. Simpler layout than other divisions.

expert-profile.migrationNotes: Older URL pattern for experts. Same content as knowledge-expert but different URL structure. Set up redirects from /expert/* to /knowledge/experts/* if consolidating. Uses sidebar section style.

contact.description: Contact page with HubSpot form and office locations
contact.migrationNotes: Compose contact layout from Columns block: col1 = HubSpot form (inline or fragment), col2 = office location cards authored as default content (image, name, address, phone). No dedicated contact-locations block needed.

knowledge.description: Knowledge Centre hub page with expert listing and CTA
knowledge.migrationNotes: Hub page for Knowledge Centre. Content-filter block lists all knowledge resources with faceted filtering.

news.description: News listing hub with sidebar and filtered news

sequences: | sidebar: default content (news categories + media resources)

news.migrationNotes: News listing hub. Content-filter block for news with sidebar for category navigation and media pack downloads.

blog-category.migrationNotes: Single page. WordPress category archive. Minimal content. Could redirect to news hub.

client-portal.migrationNotes: Single page. Gated client portal. May need special handling or external redirect to actual client portal system.

job-listing.migrationNotes: Job listings likely pulled from external ATS (applicant tracking system). May need iframe embed or custom integration. Sidebar has careers navigation.

siteConventions.ctaPattern: Bold link = primary CTA button (EDS convention: **[Link text](url)** renders as button). Almac uses 'Find out more', 'Learn more', 'Contact us', 'Download', 'Event Details' as CTA labels.
siteConventions.imageNotes: Download all images from wp-content/uploads and smushcdn.com CDN. Convert to WebP where possible. Preserve alt text. Images served from /_media/ or /media/ path in EDS. Background images in hero blocks use picture element.
siteConventions.fontStrategy: System fonts with Google Fonts fallback. Source site uses custom fonts loaded via Google Fonts. EDS should load fonts in head.html for performance.

colorPalette.notes: Extract exact brand colors from source site CSS during block development.

navigation.structure: Mega-menu with 7 top-level items: Services (dropdown per business unit), Discovery, Diagnostics, API/Chemical, Clinical Services, Clinical Technologies, Pharma Drug, Commercial Services, Analytical. Plus utility links: About, Sustainability, Careers, Contact, Client Login, Search.
navigation.edsImplementation: Author nav in /nav page using standard EDS navigation structure. Mega-menu dropdowns authored as nested lists. Mobile hamburger menu via EDS header JS.

footer.structure: 3-column footer: col1 = quick links (Careers, About, Locations, News, Knowledge), col2 = business unit links (Discovery, Diagnostics), col3 = legal links (Privacy, Terms, Modern Slavery, Company Info) + phone numbers + social icons
footer.edsImplementation: Author footer in /footer page. Use default content sections with links.

sheets.events: Extended metadata for events (date, location, type)
sheets.knowledge: Extended metadata for knowledge resources (content-type, topic, expert)
sheets.products: Extended metadata for products (category, CAS number)

queryIndex.notes: Multiple query-index sheets needed for content-filter block variants. Each sheet corresponds to a major content collection.

knownRedirects.reason: Consolidate expert profiles to single URL pattern
WordPress blog category archive → news hub

redirects.internationalizedUrls: ~200 URLs with CJK percent-encoded slugs. Preserve original paths with redirects from encoded to decoded versions if needed.
redirects.notes: Full redirect mapping should be generated during Phase 4 as pages are migrated. Track all old WordPress URLs that change.

robotsTxt.strategy: Standard EDS robots.txt. Disallow /drafts/. Allow all crawlers for public content.
robotsTxt.notes: Copy source site crawl directives where applicable. Ensure sitemap reference points to EDS sitemap.

sitemap.strategy: Auto-generated EDS sitemap from published pages. Replace 11 sub-site sitemaps with unified sitemap.

migrationPriorityOrder.rationale: Priority based on: (1) structural complexity for testing blocks, (2) page volume for maximum impact, (3) content-filter dependency chain. Start with simple high-volume archetypes to validate core blocks, then tackle complex listing pages.

phases.name: Foundation — Core blocks + simple archetypes
phases.notes: Build and validate all core Block Collection blocks. Homepage is the showcase page. Award pages are the simplest content archetype.

phases.name: Division landing pages
phases.notes: All 8 division sub-sites share similar layouts. Build remaining Block Collection blocks (quote, carousel, modal, table). Full-width-image and dark section styles.

phases.name: Profile pages
phases.notes: Build custom profile block. Expert-profile → knowledge-expert redirects. BD-team variant.

phases.name: Article/detail pages with sidebar
phases.notes: Sidebar section style is the key new capability. High volume — news-post alone is 677 pages.

phases.name: Content collections (highest risk)
phases.notes: Content-filter is the #1 risk block. Requires query-index to be populated first. Test with events (928 pages), then extend to knowledge-library (721) and news (677). Build search block.

phases.name: Gated content + edge cases
phases.notes: Fully gated content. Stub migration — title + gating form only. Manual content review required post-migration.

pilotPages.reason: Showcase page, tests hero, stats, cards, fragment
Standard division layout with columns, video, cards
Full division hub with all section types
Profile block test, related content cards
Gated content form, expert profile section

imageHandling.namingConvention: kebab-case slug derived from original filename, preserving meaningful names
imageHandling.formats: Convert to WebP where possible; keep original format for SVGs and animated GIFs
imageHandling.altText: Preserve source alt text; flag empty alt attributes for review

metadataExtraction.title: meta[property='og:title'] || document.title || h1 text
metadataExtraction.division: inferred from URL path prefix (/clinical-services/ → Clinical Services)

htmlCleanup: Remove HubSpot tracking scripts (replaced by hubspot-form block)
Remove Slick.js carousel markup (replaced by EDS carousel)
Strip inline styles except essential ones (display:none for hidden content)
Remove data-* attributes except data-src for lazy images
Convert relative URLs to absolute, then to EDS-relative paths
Normalize heading hierarchy (ensure single h1 per page)

homepage.transformNotes: Hero carousel: each .slick-slide → one carousel row. Stats: each .c-stats__item → one stats row (number + label). Cards: grouped by parent container into separate card blocks.

division-landing.transformNotes: All division pages share same WP template. Extract hero, columns, video, and card sections. Full-width image sections: extract background-image URL from inline style.

news-post.transformNotes: Extract article body as default content. Sidebar navigation becomes default content in sidebar section. Preserve date metadata.

knowledge-library.transformNotes: Article body → default content. Expert section → profile block. Gated form → hubspot-form block with specific form ID. Related content cards auto-populated.

profile-pages.transformNotes: Photo + name + title → profile block row. Bio text → default content below block.

api-chemical-product.transformNotes: Product description → default content. Specs table → table block. Inquiry form → hubspot-form(product-inquiry). Sidebar nav → default content in sidebar section.

events.transformNotes: Listing pages (events/, events-archive/) → content-filter block + heading. Detail pages → default content with date/location metadata. Carousel featured events → carousel block.

riskRegister.title: content-filter block complexity
riskRegister.description: The content-filter block serves 2,330+ listing pages across events (928), news (677), and knowledge (721). It requires fetching from query-index, client-side filtering, pagination, and URL state management. This is the most complex custom block.
riskRegister.mitigation: Use Block Party Content Filter Hub as reference architecture. Build iteratively: events first, then extend. Have fallback plan: static card lists without filtering if dynamic filtering proves too complex.

riskRegister.title: Gated careers-resource content
riskRegister.description: 36 careers-resource pages are fully gated behind HubSpot forms. Sample scrape returned no content. Actual page content is invisible to migration tooling.
riskRegister.mitigation: Stub migration with title + gating form. Post-migration manual content review required. Flag these pages in qa-report for human verification.

riskRegister.title: HubSpot form integration dependency
riskRegister.description: HubSpot forms appear on ~3,500 of 3,605 pages. Forms require external HubSpot JS API and specific portal/form IDs. Forms must render correctly and submit data to HubSpot CRM.
riskRegister.mitigation: Extract portal ID and form IDs from source site during migration. Build hubspot-form block with lazy-loading in delayed.js. Site-wide form as Fragment to reduce duplication. Test form submission in staging.

riskRegister.title: Slick.js carousel → EDS carousel conversion
riskRegister.description: Source site uses Slick.js for hero carousel (homepage), testimonial rotation, and event featured carousel. EDS Block Collection carousel has different markup and behavior.
riskRegister.mitigation: Block Collection Carousel is well-tested. Map each Slick slide to a carousel row. May lose some animations/transitions but core functionality preserved. Test autoplay, touch swipe, accessibility.

riskRegister.title: WordPress multisite URL structure
riskRegister.description: 11 sub-sites with separate WordPress installs. Some URLs have sub-site path prefixes that may not map cleanly to EDS flat structure. Image URLs reference sub-site wp-content paths.
riskRegister.mitigation: Maintain existing URL structure in EDS (e.g., /clinical-services/*, /diagnostics/*). Rewrite image URLs to EDS media paths. Generate comprehensive redirect spreadsheet.

riskRegister.title: Internationalized URLs (CJK characters)
riskRegister.description: ~200 URLs contain percent-encoded CJK characters (Chinese, Japanese, Korean). These are localized content pages, primarily in clinical-services and knowledge-library.
riskRegister.mitigation: Preserve percent-encoded URLs as-is in EDS slugs. Test that EDS routing handles encoded characters correctly. Add redirects for any URLs that need normalization.

riskRegister.title: 404 pages in sitemap
riskRegister.description: Phase 1 found stale/removed URLs in WordPress sitemaps returning 404. These are ghost entries from deleted content.
riskRegister.mitigation: Skip 404 pages during migration. Log them in qa-report. Add redirects to closest valid page or 404 handler.

riskRegister.title: Mega-menu navigation complexity
riskRegister.description: Header has 60+ deduped links in a mega-menu with multi-level dropdowns per business unit. EDS navigation structure may not support the same depth/complexity natively.
riskRegister.mitigation: Author nav in EDS using nested list structure. May need custom header JS for mega-menu dropdown behavior. Test mobile hamburger menu for all levels.

riskRegister.title: Client portal and job listing external dependencies
riskRegister.description: Client portal (/client-login/) and job listing (/careers/careers/) pages likely embed external systems (client portal app, ATS job board) via iframes or custom integration.
riskRegister.mitigation: Preserve iframe embeds or redirect to external systems. These pages may need manual post-migration setup.

buildOrder.notes: Adapt Block Collection hero. Brand gradient overlay CSS. Single-image default variant.
Adapt Block Collection cards. Add 6 variant CSS: default, news, resource, event, related, icon.
Block Collection columns as-is. Minor CSS for brand styling.
Auto-blocked. Build URL→label mapping logic. Inject in scripts.js.
Block Collection carousel. Test with hero slides and testimonials.
Custom block. Lazy-load HubSpot Forms API in delayed.js. Portal ID + Form ID config. Create /fragments/hubspot-cta for site-wide form.
Custom block. Animated counter as progressive enhancement. IntersectionObserver.
Custom block. Photo + name + title layout. Expert and BD-team variants via CSS class.
Block Collection search. Integrate with query-index.
CRITICAL. Custom block. Reference: Block Party Content Filter Hub. Query-index fetch, client-side filtering, pagination, URL state. Build events variant first.

sharedUtilities.purpose: Lazy-load HubSpot Forms API, Google Analytics, other third-party scripts
Auto-block breadcrumbs, load header/footer, LCP optimization, section style application
Global Almac brand styles: colors, typography, button patterns, section styles (sidebar, dark, grey, cta-band, full-width-image)

fragments.purpose: Site-wide 8-field HubSpot contact/inquiry form. Referenced via Fragment block on ~3,500 pages.
Social media links if reused across footer sections.

summary.criticalPath: hero → cards → carousel → hubspot-form → profile → sidebar(style) → content-filter
keyDecisions: Eliminated contact-locations block — compose from columns + cards + fragment
Eliminated icon-grid block — implement as cards(icon) variant
Eliminated sidebar block — implement as section style using CSS Grid
Site-wide HubSpot form as Fragment to avoid 3,500× duplication
Content-filter uses Block Party reference, not built from scratch
Breadcrumbs auto-blocked from URL hierarchy, no author input needed
careers-resource (36 pages) stub-migrated due to full gating
